plan.title: Profiler and Benchmarking Infrastructure

review_changelog.change: FIXED: "data_loading" record_function wrapped device transfer (lines 293-296), NOT the DataLoader iteration. Renamed to "data_to_device". DataLoader stall time is captured automatically by torch.profiler CPU activity tracing between named regions. Added "zero_grad" and "metrics_update" regions for completeness.

FIXED: schedule(wait=0, warmup=1, active=N-1) produces 0 active steps when N=1. Changed to schedule(wait=0, warmup=0, active=N). CUDA kernels are already JIT-compiled by model instantiation, so warmup is unnecessary at epoch granularity. Config validation changed from epochs>=1 to epochs>=1 only when enabled=True.

ADDED: Explicit documentation that prof.step() is per-EPOCH (not per-batch). Each profiled epoch's Chrome trace contains all batches. This is a design decision for simpler schedule semantics and smaller trace file count.

FIXED: ProfilingConfig is now STANDALONE — NOT a field on TrainingConfig. Constructed from config_dict.get("profiling", {}) in train_one_fold_task() and passed separately to build_profiler_context(). This avoids coupling infrastructure instrumentation to scientific hyperparameters.
FIXED: ProfilingConfig defined in models.py (consistent with all other config classes), NOT in separate profiling.py. Removed unnecessary file split.
ADDED: Update debug experiment configs to include "- override /profiling: debug" in their defaults lists.
FIXED: train_one_fold_task() now explicitly constructs ProfilingConfig from config_dict and passes it as separate argument to trainer.fit().
FIXED: Split metric prefixes: prof_ for profiler, diag_ww_ for WeightWatcher, sys_bench_ for GPU benchmark cache. Avoids conflating three different concerns under one prefix.

ADDED: Gzip compression for Chrome traces (.json.gz). Size gate: traces exceeding 50 MB uncompressed skip upload and log warning + summary only. Traces written to checkpoint_dir/profiling/ (volume-mounted), cleaned up after MLflow upload.
ADDED: DuckDB extraction pipeline update — prof_* and diag_* metrics excluded from training_metrics table via prefix filter. Added to T1.3 scope.

FIXED: log_profiling_artifacts() method receives pre-computed data (trace_paths, key_averages_text, summary dict), NOT the profiler object. Keeps ExperimentTracker decoupled from torch.profiler internals.
FIXED: Artifact paths split — profiling/ for torch.profiler outputs, diagnostics/ for pre-training checks and WeightWatcher.
ADDED: Additional validate_epoch record_function regions: val_data_to_device, val_forward, val_extended_metrics. Covers both code paths (with/without val_roi_size).
ADDED: ProfilingSummary.validation_profiled bool field. Fraction metrics documentation notes that validation time is excluded when _skip_all_val is True.
FIXED: total_profiled_epochs counted at runtime via on_trace_ready callback counter, not derived from config. Handles early stopping correctly.
FIXED: CUDA memory metrics renamed to prof_cuda_peak_allocated_mb and prof_cuda_peak_reserved_mb. Disambiguates from nvidia-smi readings (sys_gpu_memory_used_mb) and MLflow system metrics.

RENAMED: T2.3 test from "test_diagnostics_skipped_when_profiling_disabled" to "test_diagnostics_run_even_when_profiling_disabled". Diagnostics always run (they are free); ProfilingConfig.enabled controls only torch.profiler.

phase.name: Config Infrastructure
phase.description:
Create ProfilingConfig Pydantic model and Hydra config group. This is the foundation — all other tasks read profiling settings from this config. Default: profiling enabled, 5 profiled epochs for real runs, 2 for debug.
ARCHITECTURAL DECISION (RC4): ProfilingConfig is STANDALONE — not a field on TrainingConfig. It is constructed from config_dict.get("profiling", {}) in train_one_fold_task() and passed separately to build_profiler_context(). Rationale: profiling is infrastructure instrumentation, not a scientific hyperparameter. Coupling it to TrainingConfig pollutes the training contract.

task.issue_title: feat(config): add Hydra profiling config group + ProfilingConfig model
task.description:
Create ProfilingConfig Pydantic model in src/minivess/config/models.py (after CheckpointConfig, BEFORE TrainingConfig — NOT as a field on TrainingConfig). ProfilingConfig is standalone: constructed separately from config_dict in train_one_fold_task().
Create configs/profiling/{default,debug,disabled}.yaml (NO @package declaration — Hydra config group files default to their group name).
Update configs/base.yaml defaults list with "- profiling: default".
Update debug experiment configs to include "- override /profiling: debug".
Config fields: - enabled: bool (default True — profiling is core infrastructure) - epochs: int (default 5 — how many epochs to actively profile) - activities: list[str] (default ["cpu", "cuda"]) - profile_memory: bool (default True) - record_shapes: bool (default False — adds ~5-10% overhead) - with_flops: bool (default True — low overhead, high value) - with_stack: bool (default False — adds ~10-15% overhead) - export_chrome_trace: bool (default True) - compress_traces: bool (default True — gzip Chrome trace JSON) - trace_size_limit_mb: int (default 50 — skip upload if larger)
Validation: epochs >= 1 enforced only when enabled=True (via model_validator). When enabled=False, epochs value is irrelevant.

tdd_spec.red: - tests/v2/unit/test_profiling_config.py::TestProfilingConfig::test_defaults → ProfilingConfig().enabled is True, epochs==5, activities==["cpu","cuda"] - test_profiling_config_disabled → ProfilingConfig(enabled=False, epochs=0) is valid (no validation error) - test_profiling_config_enabled_epochs_ge_1 → ProfilingConfig(enabled=True, epochs=0) raises ValidationError - test_profiling_config_is_not_on_training_config → TrainingConfig has NO "profiling" attribute (standalone) - test_profiling_config_compress_traces_default_true → ProfilingConfig().compress_traces is True - test_base_yaml_includes_profiling_default → configs/base.yaml has "profiling: default" in defaults list - test_debug_configs_override_profiling → debug_single_model.yaml has "override /profiling: debug" in defaults - test_profiling_debug_yaml_epochs_2 → configs/profiling/debug.yaml has epochs: 2 - test_profiling_disabled_yaml → configs/profiling/disabled.yaml has enabled: false - test_compose_experiment_resolves_profiling → compose_experiment_config() result has "profiling" key
tdd_spec.green: - Add ProfilingConfig to src/minivess/config/models.py (standalone class) - Add ProfilingConfig to src/minivess/config/__init__.py exports - Create configs/profiling/default.yaml (enabled: true, epochs: 5) - Create configs/profiling/debug.yaml (enabled: true, epochs: 2) - Create configs/profiling/disabled.yaml (enabled: false) - Add "- profiling: default" to configs/base.yaml defaults list - Add "- override /profiling: debug" to all debug experiment configs - Modify train_one_fold_task() to extract ProfilingConfig from config_dict

phase.name: PyTorch Profiler Core
phase.description:
Integrate torch.profiler into trainer.py training loop. Seven named regions provide per-operation visibility in training, plus four in validation. The profiler runs for the first N epochs via schedule(), then exports gzip-compressed Chrome traces and summary tables to MLflow.
DESIGN DECISION (RC1, RC3): record_function regions label device transfer as "data_to_device" (NOT "data_loading"). DataLoader I/O stall time is captured automatically by torch.profiler CPU activity tracing between named regions. prof.step() is called per-EPOCH, not per-batch.

task.issue_title: feat(profiling): add record_function annotations to trainer.py
task.description:
Add torch.profiler.record_function context managers to SegmentationTrainer. record_function is a no-op when profiler is not active (zero overhead).
Training loop regions (train_epoch, 7 regions): 1. "zero_grad" — optimizer.zero_grad() (line 300) 2. "data_to_device" — batch dict .to(device) + key extraction (lines 293-298) 3. "forward" — self.model(images) inside autocast (line 305) 4. "loss_compute" — self._compute_loss() inside autocast (line 306) 5. "backward_optimizer" — scaler.backward + clip + step (lines 315-323) 6. "metrics_update" — self.metrics.update() under no_grad (lines 328-330)
NOTE: DataLoader stall time (actual I/O bottleneck) is NOT wrapped by record_function. It appears automatically as CPU idle time between "backward_optimizer" end and "data_to_device" start in the profiler trace. This is the standard PyTorch profiler approach for detecting data starvation.
Validation loop regions (validate_epoch, 4 regions): 1. "val_data_to_device" — batch dict .to(device) (lines 368-373) 2. "val_forward" — sliding_window_inference OR direct model(images) (lines 384-409) 3. "val_loss_compute" — loss computation in validation 4. "val_extended_metrics" — extended metrics computation (lines 417-440)

tdd_spec.red: - tests/v2/unit/test_profiler_annotations.py::test_train_epoch_has_record_function_regions → AST-parse trainer.py, verify 6 record_function calls in train_epoch (Use ast.walk, NOT regex — Rule #16) - test_validate_epoch_has_record_function_regions → AST-parse trainer.py, verify 4 record_function calls in validate_epoch - test_record_function_region_names_train → Names match {"zero_grad","data_to_device","forward","loss_compute", "backward_optimizer","metrics_update"} - test_record_function_region_names_val → Names match {"val_data_to_device","val_forward","val_loss_compute", "val_extended_metrics"} - test_data_to_device_not_named_data_loading → No record_function("data_loading") in trainer.py (banned name)
tdd_spec.green: - Import record_function from torch.profiler at top of trainer.py - Add 6 record_function wrappers in train_epoch() - Add 4 record_function wrappers in validate_epoch()

task.issue_title: feat(profiling): integrate torch.profiler.profile context in fit()
task.description:
Wrap the epoch loop in SegmentationTrainer.fit() with torch.profiler.profile context manager. When profiling disabled, use contextlib.nullcontext().
Create src/minivess/pipeline/profiler_integration.py with: - build_profiler_context(config: ProfilingConfig, output_dir: Path) → ctx mgr - TraceHandler class — on_trace_ready callback that: (a) Saves Chrome trace to output_dir/profiling/ (volume-mounted checkpoint dir) (b) Applies gzip compression if config.compress_traces is True (c) Enforces trace_size_limit_mb gate (skip if too large, log warning) (d) Increments runtime epoch counter (for total_profiled_epochs) (e) Cleans up uncompressed trace after compression - ProfilingSummary dataclass with fields: trace_paths: list[Path], overhead_pct: float, total_profiled_epochs: int, validation_profiled: bool, key_averages_text: str, summary_dict: dict
SCHEDULE DESIGN (RC2): schedule(wait=0, warmup=0, active=N) — no warmup. CUDA kernels are JIT-compiled during model instantiation, so epoch-level warmup is unnecessary. prof.step() is called per-EPOCH (RC3).
fit() receives ProfilingConfig as separate parameter (RC4): def fit(self, train_loader, val_loader, *, profiling_config=None, ...): profiling_config = profiling_config or ProfilingConfig(enabled=False) ctx = build_profiler_context(profiling_config, checkpoint_dir) with ctx as prof: for epoch in range(self.config.max_epochs): ... existing code ... if prof is not None: prof.step()

tdd_spec.red: - tests/v2/unit/test_profiler_integration.py::test_build_profiler_context_enabled → Returns torch.profiler.profile instance when config.enabled=True - test_build_profiler_context_disabled → Returns contextlib.nullcontext when config.enabled=False - test_trace_handler_saves_chrome_trace_gzipped → TraceHandler writes .json.gz file to output_dir/profiling/ - test_trace_handler_skips_large_traces → Trace exceeding trace_size_limit_mb → warning logged, file skipped - test_trace_handler_counts_profiled_epochs → After 3 on_trace_ready calls, handler.profiled_epoch_count == 3 - test_profiler_schedule_active_equals_config_epochs → schedule(wait=0, warmup=0, active=N) matches config - test_profiling_summary_has_validation_profiled_field → ProfilingSummary.validation_profiled is bool - test_fit_accepts_profiling_config_parameter → fit() has profiling_config keyword argument - test_early_stopping_produces_partial_profiling → Early stop at epoch 3 with epochs=5 → total_profiled_epochs==3
tdd_spec.green: - Create src/minivess/pipeline/profiler_integration.py - Implement build_profiler_context() with schedule(wait=0, warmup=0, active=N) - Implement TraceHandler with gzip + size gate + epoch counter - Implement ProfilingSummary dataclass - Modify fit() to accept profiling_config parameter - Wrap epoch loop with profiler context - Add prof.step() at end of each epoch iteration

task.description:
Add log_profiling_artifacts() method to ExperimentTracker in tracking.py. Method receives PRE-COMPUTED data (RC11) — NOT the profiler object:
def log_profiling_artifacts( self, *, trace_paths: list[Path], # .json.gz files on disk key_averages_text: str, # pre-formatted table string summary: dict[str, object], # serializable summary dict memory_stats: dict[int, dict] | None = None, # epoch → stats ) → None
MLflow artifact structure: profiling/ (NOT diagnostics/ — see RC12) ├── chrome_trace_epoch_{n}.json.gz (gzipped per profiled epoch) ├── key_averages.txt (top-20 ops by CUDA time) └── summary.json (wall times, overhead, trace sizes)
MLflow metrics (per-run summary, NOT per-epoch): - prof_overhead_pct: (profiled_epoch_time - unprofiled_epoch_time) / unprofiled - prof_trace_size_mb: total compressed trace file size - prof_data_to_device_fraction: data_to_device time / total step time - prof_forward_fraction: forward time / total step time - prof_backward_fraction: backward_optimizer time / total step time - prof_mixed_precision_enabled: bool (log for context, RC7-low)
MLflow params (profiling config, logged once per run): - prof_cfg_enabled, prof_cfg_epochs, prof_cfg_profile_memory, etc.
DuckDB exclusion (RC10): Update duckdb_extraction.py metric routing to exclude prof_* and diag_* prefixes from the training_metrics table.
Cleanup: After successful upload, remove trace files from checkpoint_dir/profiling/ to avoid wasting Docker volume storage (RC9).

tdd_spec.red: - tests/v2/unit/test_profiling_mlflow.py::test_log_profiling_artifacts_creates_summary → Mock MLflow client, verify log_artifact called with "profiling/summary.json" - test_log_profiling_artifacts_logs_overhead_metric → Verify mlflow.log_metric called with "prof_overhead_pct" - test_log_profiling_artifacts_logs_fraction_metrics → Verify prof_data_to_device_fraction, prof_forward_fraction logged - test_profiling_summary_json_schema → Summary JSON has required keys: wall_times, overhead_pct, trace_sizes, validation_profiled, mixed_precision_enabled - test_key_averages_table_logged_as_text → Verify "profiling/key_averages.txt" artifact logged - test_profiling_config_logged_as_params → Verify prof_cfg_enabled, prof_cfg_epochs logged as params - test_trace_files_cleaned_up_after_upload → After log_profiling_artifacts(), trace files no longer on disk - test_method_does_not_import_torch_profiler → AST-parse tracking.py: no import of torch.profiler (decoupled) - test_duckdb_extraction_excludes_prof_metrics → duckdb_extraction.py metric routing skips prof_* and diag_* prefixes
tdd_spec.green: - Add log_profiling_artifacts() to ExperimentTracker (pre-computed data signature) - Implement compressed trace upload loop - Implement key_averages text artifact logging - Implement summary.json creation - Log prof_cfg_* params from ProfilingConfig - Log per-run fraction + overhead metrics - Add cleanup step (unlink trace files after upload) - Update duckdb_extraction.py: add prof_ and diag_ to exclusion filter

phase.name: Pre/Post-Training Diagnostics
phase.description: Structured model diagnostics beyond loss curves. Pre-training sanity checks catch broken models before wasting GPU hours. Post-training WeightWatcher provides spectral health analysis. Diagnostics ALWAYS run regardless of ProfilingConfig.enabled (they are free — RC17). Artifacts under diagnostics/.

task.description:
Create pre-training sanity checks module following torch-test patterns. Run BEFORE training loop starts. Six checks: 1. Model output shape mismatch 2. Gradient flow (at least one param has .grad after backward) 3. Loss sanity (random init loss near expected value) 4. Data range (inputs in [0,1] or [-1,1]) 5. Label integrity (valid class indices) 6. NaN/Inf check (no NaN or Inf in model output)
Artifact: diagnostics/pre_training_checks.json (NOT profiling/ — RC12). Failures at severity="error" abort training with clear message.

tdd_spec.red: - tests/v2/unit/test_pre_training_checks.py::test_output_shape_check_passes - test_output_shape_check_fails_wrong_channels - test_gradient_flow_check_passes - test_gradient_flow_check_fails_frozen - test_loss_sanity_check_passes - test_nan_check_fails_on_nan_output - test_run_all_checks_returns_list_of_results - test_artifact_path_is_diagnostics_not_profiling → Verify artifact logged to "diagnostics/pre_training_checks.json"
tdd_spec.green: - Create src/minivess/diagnostics/__init__.py - Create src/minivess/diagnostics/pre_training_checks.py - Implement CheckResult dataclass + 6 checks + orchestrator - Log artifact to diagnostics/ path

task.description:
Integrate WeightWatcher spectral analysis as post-training diagnostic. Filter to trainable layers only (exclude frozen SAM3 ViT backbone).
Metrics (RC8 — diag_ww_ prefix, NOT prof_ww_): - diag_ww_alpha_mean, diag_ww_alpha_std - diag_ww_num_layers_analyzed
Artifact: diagnostics/weightwatcher_per_layer.json (NOT profiling/ — RC12).

tdd_spec.red: - tests/v2/unit/test_weightwatcher_diagnostics.py::test_analyze_model_returns_summary - test_analyze_filters_frozen_layers - test_mlflow_metrics_logged_with_diag_prefix → Verify diag_ww_alpha_mean metric logged (NOT prof_ww_) - test_per_layer_artifact_under_diagnostics → Verify diagnostics/weightwatcher_per_layer.json artifact logged
tdd_spec.green: - Create src/minivess/diagnostics/weight_diagnostics.py - Implement run_weightwatcher(model, tracker) - Filter to trainable layers via requires_grad - Log diag_ww_* metrics + diagnostics/ artifact

task.issue_title: feat(diagnostics): integrate pre/post checks into train_flow.py
task.description: Wire pre-training checks and WeightWatcher into the training flow. Diagnostics ALWAYS run regardless of ProfilingConfig.enabled (RC17).

tdd_spec.red: - tests/v2/unit/test_train_flow_diagnostics.py::test_pre_checks_called_before_fit - test_pre_check_error_aborts_flow - test_weightwatcher_called_after_fit - test_diagnostics_run_even_when_profiling_disabled (RC17) → ProfilingConfig(enabled=False) → both diagnostics still run
tdd_spec.green: - Add run_pre_training_checks() call after model build - Add run_weightwatcher() call after fit() - Both run unconditionally (not gated by ProfilingConfig.enabled)

phase.name: GPU Benchmark Container
phase.description: Run-once GPU capability benchmarking. Produces YAML cache with per-model throughput, peak VRAM, and feasibility flags. Cache invalidated on driver/CUDA version change. Directly closes issue #564. Benchmark params logged with sys_bench_ prefix (RC8).

task.issue_title: feat(benchmark): GPU benchmark script + cache YAML
task.description:
GPU benchmark module. Normalizes GPU model name to canonical form (lowercase, strip "NVIDIA GeForce" prefix) for stable regression detection filtering (RC11-MLflow-low).
YAML cache includes: schema_version, instance info, per-model benchmarks (peak_vram, throughput, forward_ms), capabilities (binary feasibility), max_batch_size estimates.

tdd_spec.red: - tests/v2/unit/test_gpu_benchmark.py::test_benchmark_yaml_schema_version - test_benchmark_yaml_has_instance_section - test_benchmark_yaml_has_capabilities - test_cache_invalidation_on_driver_change - test_benchmark_result_dataclass - test_feasibility_check_logic - test_gpu_model_name_normalized → "NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2070 SUPER" → "rtx_2070_super"
tdd_spec.green: - Create src/minivess/compute/gpu_benchmark.py - Implement probe_gpu_info() with name normalization - Implement per-model micro-benchmarks - Implement write_benchmark_yaml() + is_cache_valid()

task.description: Dedicated benchmark Docker image and compose service.

tdd_spec.green: - Create deployment/docker/Dockerfile.benchmark - Add gpu-benchmark service to docker-compose.flows.yml - Add benchmark_data volume

task.issue_title: feat(benchmark): benchmark cache integration with training flow
task.description: Training flow reads GPU benchmark cache. Params logged with sys_bench_ prefix (RC8), NOT prof_gpu_: - sys_bench_gpu_model_normalized (canonical name for filtering) - sys_bench_throughput_{model} (images/sec) - sys_bench_vram_peak_{model} (peak MB) - sys_bench_feasible_{model} (bool)

tdd_spec.red: - tests/v2/unit/test_benchmark_cache_integration.py::test_valid_cache_logged_to_mlflow - test_missing_cache_logs_warning - test_invalid_cache_ignored - test_benchmark_params_have_sys_bench_prefix → All logged params start with "sys_bench_" (NOT "prof_gpu_")
tdd_spec.green: - Create src/minivess/compute/gpu_profile.py - Implement load_benchmark_cache() + log_benchmark_to_mlflow() - Wire into train_flow.py before trainer.fit()

phase.name: Memory Stats and Regression Audit Trail
phase.description: Per-epoch CUDA memory statistics and performance regression detection. Every training run produces a performance fingerprint in MLflow for root-cause analysis of performance drops.

task.issue_title: feat(profiling): CUDA memory stats per-epoch logging
task.description: Log torch.cuda.memory_stats() at end of each profiled epoch. Metrics use prof_cuda_ prefix (RC16) to disambiguate from nvidia-smi readings (sys_gpu_memory_used_mb) and MLflow system metrics: - prof_cuda_peak_allocated_mb (torch allocator peak) - prof_cuda_peak_reserved_mb (torch allocator reserved) - prof_cuda_alloc_retries (fragmentation indicator)

tdd_spec.red: - tests/v2/unit/test_cuda_memory_logging.py::test_memory_stats_extracted - test_memory_stats_logged_per_epoch → Verify log_epoch_metrics with prof_cuda_peak_allocated_mb - test_memory_stats_skipped_on_cpu - test_fragmentation_warning
tdd_spec.green: - Create src/minivess/diagnostics/cuda_memory.py - Implement extract_memory_stats() → dict - Integrate into trainer.py epoch loop

task.description: Utility functions for detecting performance regressions from MLflow profiling history. Compares latest run against baseline (median of last 5 runs on same GPU). Uses sys_bench_gpu_model_normalized for GPU matching (avoids driver-version string variations).

tdd_spec.red: - tests/v2/unit/test_regression_detection.py::test_no_regression_within_threshold - test_regression_detected_above_threshold - test_baseline_from_empty_history - test_regression_report_format - test_gpu_matching_uses_normalized_name → get_profiling_baseline filters by sys_bench_gpu_model_normalized
tdd_spec.green: - Create src/minivess/diagnostics/regression_detection.py - Implement BaselineStats, RegressionResult dataclasses - Implement get_profiling_baseline() with normalized GPU name filter - Implement detect_regression() + format_regression_report()

step.description: Config infrastructure — all other tasks depend on this
Profiler context in fit() — depends on T0.1 + T1.1
MLflow artifact logging + DuckDB exclusion — depends on T1.2
Pre-checks + WeightWatcher — independent, buildable in parallel
Wire diagnostics into train_flow — depends on T2.1+T2.2
GPU benchmark script — can start after Phase 0
Dockerfile + cache integration — buildable in parallel

plan.dependency_graph:
T0.1 (config) ──> T1.1 (annotations) ──> T1.2 (profiler ctx) ──> T1.3 (MLflow+DuckDB) | | | T2.1 (pre-checks) ────────┤ | T2.2 (WeightWatcher) ─────┤ | v | T2.3 (wire into flow) | └──> T3.1 (benchmark) ──> T3.2 (Docker) ──┐ T3.3 (cache) ──┘
T4.1 (memory stats) ────── (after T1.2) T4.2 (regression) ─────── (after T1.3)

risk.title: Profiler overhead exceeds budget
risk.mitigation: Budget: <=10% per profiled epoch, <=5% amortized. schedule() limits active profiling to N epochs. Default with_stack=False and record_shapes=False. Measure prof_overhead_pct metric. Emergency: profiling=disabled override.

risk.title: Chrome trace file size for large models
risk.mitigation: Gzip compression (RC9) achieves ~10x reduction. Size gate: traces exceeding trace_size_limit_mb (default 50 MB uncompressed) skip upload. Traces written to volume-mounted checkpoint_dir, cleaned up after MLflow upload.

risk.title: DuckDB pipeline pollution from prof_* metrics
risk.mitigation: Metric routing in duckdb_extraction.py excludes prof_* and diag_* prefixes from training_metrics table (RC10). Added as explicit scope of T1.3.

risk.title: WeightWatcher on frozen backbones
risk.mitigation: Filter to trainable layers via requires_grad check. Log diag_ww_num_layers_analyzed vs total for transparency.

risk.title: GPU benchmark cache stale after driver update
risk.mitigation: Cache key includes driver_version and cuda_version. Automatic re-run on mismatch. GPU model name normalized for stable comparison.

risk.title: ProfilingConfig breaks existing experiment configs
risk.mitigation: ProfilingConfig is standalone (not on TrainingConfig). All-defaults constructor. Existing configs without profiling group get default (enabled=True, epochs=5).

phase.description: PyTorch Profiler core (annotations + context + MLflow + DuckDB)
